seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Claude's tool use enables real-time conversational AI that processes speech, reasons with tools, and responds via voice, powering applications like virtual assistants. However, the high-stakes, sub-second latency environment amplifies risks like tool chain failures, incoherent responses, and safety violations. Vex guardrails ensure reliability by verifying response appropriateness, latency, coherence, and safety in this critical pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: microphone audio streams to a speech-to-text (STT) service like Deepgram or Whisper for transcription. The transcribed text feeds into Claude's tool use API, where the model reasons, calls tools (e.g., database queries, APIs), and generates responses. The final text output converts to speech via TTS (e.g., ElevenLabs) and plays back, all within sub-second latency budgets.

Claude's agentic tool use handles complex reasoning chains, parallel tool calls, and computer use for screen interactions, but introduces failure modes like cascading errors or inconsistent reasoning. Vex wraps these interactions to verify outputs against voice-specific criteria.

The pipeline requires <300ms end-to-end latency, making verification lightweight and async-critical.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Mic → [STT: Deepgram/Whisper] → [Claude Tool Use + Vex Verify] → [TTS: ElevenLabs] → Speaker
                    ↓
              [Tools: DB/API/Computer Use]

Latency Budget: STT(100ms) + LLM(150ms) + TTS(50ms) = <300ms

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early tool call errors (e.g., invalid API params) propagate through reasoning chains, producing unusable voice responses
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each tool_use response before chain continuation

risks.name: Latency Budget Violations
risks.description: Tool chains or extended thinking exceed 300ms, breaking real-time voice interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces strict latency assertions on responses

risks.name: Conversation Incoherence
risks.description: Parallel tool calls return conflicting data, leading to nonsensical voice output
risks.mitigation: Vex checks response coherence against conversation history

risks.name: Safety Filter Bypass
risks.description: Computer use tools perform unintended screen actions during voice sessions
risks.mitigation: Vex safety filtering blocks harmful tool actions and responses

implementation.title: Real-time STT to Claude Pipeline
implementation.description: Stream audio to Deepgram STT, pipe transcript to Claude with tool use for voice agents
implementation.code:
import anthropic, deepgram, asyncio

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
deepgram = Deepgram('key')

async def voice_pipeline(audio_chunk):
    transcript = await deepgram.transcription(audio_chunk)
    msg = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": transcript}],
        tools=[{"name": "query_db", "input_schema": {...}}]
    )
    return msg.content[0].text
